status_breakdown.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms

events_with_summaries.title: Identifying the correct Styrofoam type for recycling
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Backroom: Sending the wrong Styrofoam to Used Asset Centers increases costs and risks contaminating recyclable materials. Use a simple bend test at the store – rigid pieces should be returned, while flexible pieces should be disposed of on site.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept 12: Compliance Requirement for Paint Service Desk
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Dept. 12: Install the Paint Service Desk disclosure sign upon arrival in a visible, customer-facing spot and keep it unobstructed.

events_with_summaries.title: Walmart Protection Plan - New Quick Reference Barcode Sheet
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Front end: Allstate has updated the Walmart Protection Plan Quick Reference Barcode Sheets for store registers. The new version no longer displays prices so they can be used for an unlimited amount of time.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 40: Continue to Feature Equate Allergy
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In department 40, continue to set Equate Allergy features which are shipping to stores at the start of each month. Quantities are calibrated to support an 80 percent sell through target.  Most markets are past peak allergy season and moving into the back half, while the Northeast is just beginning to ramp up.

events_with_summaries.title: Item Info Is Now in Pinpoint
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Consumables and Hardlines: Item Info is now built directly into Pinpoint, so you can view item details and purchase history without leaving the app. This helps you resolve exceptions faster without switching devices or tools.

events_with_summaries.title: Don’t Let Sales Slip This Mother’s Day!
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Store Fulfillment: Mother’s Day demand is building, so now is the time to stay focused on the biggest sales drivers. Keep floral fresh and full, make sure greeting cards are organized and easy to shop, maintain strong in-stocks on giftables like candles and spa sets, and use chocolate and sweets as add-on opportunities near floral and cards. Above all, keep the salesfloor full, clean and shoppable to capture both planned and impulse purchases.

events_with_summaries.title: FY27 InHome Driver Safety Calendar
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Store Fulfillment: The FY27 InHome Driver Safety Calendar gives stores and drivers a year-long plan to stay focused on safe delivery practices. Each month features a safety theme based on real-world risks and Lytx insights, with clear actions and in-app messages to reinforce key behaviors. Stores should print the calendar from the Safety Enterprise Hub and post it in the InHome backroom for daily visibility.

events_with_summaries.title: InHome In-Cab Camera Insight for Distracted Driving
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Store Fulfillment: Lytx in-cab camera alerts help remind InHome drivers to stay focused and avoid distracted driving. Keep your eyes on the road, your hands on the wheel and your phone out of reach. If you hear an alert, refocus right away. Safe driving protects you, your load and everyone on the road.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 72: Cut the Cord Cover Letter Correction
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Dept. 72: Disregard the cover letter instruction to remove or destroy Roku signage, and keep Roku displays and backing in place as shown on the floorplan.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 29: Sleepwear Mod Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: We're asking stores to verify that sleepwear mods are set correctly in Department 29.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 91: Frozen Modular Update
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Department 91: Associates should make sure Frozen pizza boxes are standing up and facing forward. This will help increase sales.

Department 91: Associates should make sure all Frozen features are set to help drive sales.

Department 91: In week 10 (April 4-10), four ice cream novelty innovations were launched including iconic brands, Walmart exclusives and fun options to drive customer excitement and future trips. These items include SNICKERS and M&M's Minis Ice Cream Bar, Nutella® Ice Cream Cones and Pints, FATBOY Strawberry Cheesecake Ice Cream Sandwich Pops and Drumstick® Red, White & Blueberry Sundae Cones.

Department 91: In week 14, Great Value Breakfast Bowls are being added back into the Frozen Breakfast modular.

Department 91: Did you know, stores on average sell 240 units of Banquet Chicken Pot Pies per week. At $1 each, this is a great inflation buster and meal solution for customers.

Department 91: Associates should make sure these four Rollback items are flagged and available for customers: Marie Callender's Dinners, Uncrustables 4ct, 10ct and 15ct, Caulipower Bites, Pillsbury Cinnamon Rolls and Totinos Pizza Rolls.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 92: Quick Modular Update for Sauces and Condiments
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Department 92: A week 14 quick modular is being implemented for Sauces and Condiments. This will address minor opportunities, late developing new items, brand flow and overall assortment cleanup.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 92: The Great American Grill Out – Celebrating America's Birthday
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Department 92: To celebrate America's 250th birthday, stores will have a new and improved Grilling Train. Items will include buns, Coca-Cola and Frito Lay chips along with a selection of core condiments. Many of the items are on Rollback and will be a featured through May and June.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 95: The Grilling Train Will Include a 1/2 Pallet of Frito Lay Chips
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Dept. 95: As part of the America 250th celebration, the grilling train will include a half-pallet of party size potato chips from Frito Lay. It is the responsibility of the Frito Lay representative to keep this half-pallet maintained for the duration of this program.

events_with_summaries.title: Summer Grilling Train
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: The 12-week Great American Grill-Out campaign celebrates  America’s 250th birthday with a “Grilling Train.” It runs week 14 (May 2–8) through week 26 (July 25–31). Positioned in action alley for maximum visibility, it features eight high-volume items across Departments 92, 95 and 98 with sustained Rollback pricing.

events_with_summaries.title: D80: Bulk Meat & Cheese Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Department 80: Replenishment settings have been adjusted to make sure stores have one chub per facing of each item, plus one for behind the counter to slice and sell. associates need to make sure their on hands are correct in order for this replenishment goal to be met.

Department 80: To reduce markdowns, stores should only weigh and label enough product for 2-3 days of sales. Once associates label it, the product only has 5-7 days to sell before CVP.

Department 80: This message points out four known in-stock issues. The items include Jennie O Sundried Turkey, Jennie O Herb Roasted Turkey, Prima Della Honey Roasted Turkey and Prima Della Truffle Gouda.

events_with_summaries.title: D93 Week 13 Merch Message
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Ensure the sales floor is set for Cinco de Mayo (Tuesday, May 5) and Mother’s Day (Sunday, May 10). Expect a ground beef lift for Cinco de Mayo; for Mother’s Day, watch for increased demand in steaks, ribs, lobster, snow crab and other grilling items. Keep features set and modular homes full. Slack out dinner sausage on Wednesdays to build up in preparation for weekend sales. With warmer weather coming, monitor case temps: target 30 degrees; shelf life drops at 34, and each degree above 34 costs a day. See the Meat Better Way Guide for your store formats for more details.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 80: Chicken Salad Croissant Will Arrive Ready to Sell
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In the Deli (department 80), the in-store made chicken salad croissant is transitioning to a supplier-produced version that will now arrive ready to sell with no in‑store production or manual ordering required.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 94: Mother's Day Floral
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Mother’s Day is near—execute the department 94 fresh floral plan now to win the season. Use the Mother’s Day Floral Scan Sheet daily to scan items and adjust inventory. Set and price action alley displays, then aisle-locate by scanning into a nearby label. Keep displays organized, full and fresh—refill and consolidate shippers often, and update locations as product moves. With about 80% of sales in the final three days, it’s essential to assign dedicated coverage May 7–10.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 94: Produce Instock Risk
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Produce supply remains tight due to weather in Florida/Mexico and seasonal transitions. Supply constrains will be seen with carrots, chilis, cucumbers, green beans/okra, grapes, green bells, nectarines, mangos, tomatoes and watermelons. Incremental supply is being sourced and we expect recovery for most items to be between weeks 13 and 15. We ask stores to flex adjacent items to fill outs during supply gaps.

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 94: Produce Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Mother’s Day flowers are coming—receive all product, set features with correct retail prices, and pick gently to keep bouquets gift‑ready; cherry season is here, so feature big, rotate inventory for freshness, and execute retail actions (no TPAs) through June 24; and summer starts with watermelons—check your bin details and set a winning display now. Also, count juice on the Premium Juice mod with cost inventory (excluding Olipop and Poppi), verify asparagus on-hands as adverse weather constrains supply for asparagus and bulk slicing/Roma tomatoes and repurpose the Salsa Bar Bin as a temporary tomato solution, and merchandise baby broccoli upright (do not lay flat).

events_with_summaries.title: Dept. 98 & 81: WK13 Bakery & Bread Updates
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: In Commercial Bread (department 81), continue selling through markdowns and begin the week 13 Buns mod (category 103) reset. Watch for new UPCs on Artesano and remove outdated Sam’s Choice tags as product transitions. Execute Bimbo Rollbacks beginning April 27 and keep tortilla Rollbacks featured, full and priced correctly. In Fresh Bakery (department 98), continue the Mother’s Day production through the holiday and focus on volume driving features. Be sure to fill, price and maintain Rollback features on tables and the Bakery SPI where space allows: Mini Muffins, 28-count Cookie Box, Angel Food varieties. As everyday traffic rebuilds and Mother’s Day begins, be sure to fill the following: carrot cakes, cheesecakes, white decorated cakes, and angel food items. Finally, watch for temporary out of stocks on early deletes/swaps and flex adjacent items as needed.

events_with_summaries.title: OnePay Builder Card is here — a new way to help associates build credit
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Front End: On Wednesday, April 22, OnePay is launching the Builder Card, a secured credit card available to Walmart associates and customers who bank through OnePay. The Builder Card helps build credit by reporting positive secured credit card activity to major credit bureaus.

events_with_summaries.title: Update: Attendance Authorization Reason
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Beginning Saturday, May 2, when managers authorize an exception in the Global Time and Attendance system using Protected Paid Time off, an updated authorization reason will display protected by associate balance, ppto and or sick.

This update will not change ppto eligibility, accruals or how associates can protect their attendance exceptions. Review the attendance authorize reason guide for more information.

events_with_summaries.title: AI Tip of the Week: Improve the Associate Experience
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Keep calm and ask my assistant. AI Tip of the Week.

My assistant can help spot behavior gaps and take action to improve consistency, accountability and retention. My assistant can act as an expert Walmart operations leader or people partner, by entering tasks and current facility conditions. My assistant can provide a table with specific headings and data to help make performance improvements and an improved associate experience during their first 90 days.

events_with_summaries.title: Cart Safety
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Total Store: Carts are essential to daily work, but using them unsafely can put associates and customers at risk. Simple habits like maintaining clear visibility, controlling loads and staying aware in high-traffic areas help prevent injuries and disruptions. Prioritizing safe cart use supports a safer, more efficient store for everyone.

events_with_summaries.title: VPI Selection Is Now Open and Everyone’s Invited
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text: Q2 VPI item selection is currently open through Thursday, April 30, and all are welcome! Participation is voluntary and may only occur during the associate’s working hours. Associates may submit one VPI item per quarter, monetary prizes are awarded to hourly winners at the store, market and regional levels. Find full contest details, program updates and the list of winners centralized in the VPI section of the Features Better Way guide.

events_with_summaries.title: Weekly Message: Ethics – Integrity in Action
events_with_summaries.status: Review for Publish review - No Comms
events_with_summaries.summary_text:
Ethics, Integrity in Action.

If you know an associate who spoke up, prevented a compliance issue or did the right thing when it was challenging, you could nominate them for an Integrity in Action Award. Review the Integrity in Action Award page on One Walmart for additional details.
